expectations.text: Output is a single self-contained HTML file
HTML uses reveal.js 4.x via CDN
Page count is between 7 and 10 (target 8)
Content is in Chinese (Simplified)
Academic / rigorous style — NOT marketing-style hype
Includes trial registration number NCT04567890 as data source
Includes both efficacy numbers: control 34% and treatment 12%
Includes statistical evidence: p<0.001 and n=1842
Includes confidence interval visualization or explicit CI values
expectations.evidence: Tables + bar charts + forest plot HR; explicit CIs

expectations.text: Includes a methodology section / slide
Layout has no real overflow (validate.js total = 0)
expectations.evidence: validate.js reports 0 overflows. Cover screenshot is clean academic typography.

notes: P0=1 (would be blocked by skill gate), but 0 overflows and 8/8 page count
Cover is clean but generic academic — no bespoke design language
with_skill pass rate +0.13 (significant) over baseline — driven by richer content and stronger design language
with_skill P0=0 across all 3 (vs 4 P0 in baseline) — skill's hard rules prevent design rule violations

with_skill has 71 overflows (62 in security-training alone) vs baseline 52 — the 14em content budget is too tight for dense training content; fashion case (7 vs 52) shows skill actually wins when content is well-paced
with_skill undershoots page count in 2/3 cases (12 vs 15, 9 vs 12) — design depth is traded for less coverage. The agent stops adding pages too early because the budget feels tight.
Cost is steep: +194s (61% slower) and +99,889 tokens (~9x) per run

Visually (covers): with_skill security-training has stronger 'FIELD KIT' aesthetic; with_skill sustainable-fashion has the strongest lookbook of all 6; with_skill clinical-trial is clean and on-brief; baselines are generic but functional

Visual issues not caught by lint: sustainable-fashion with_skill cover has 4+ page markers stacked/overlapping at the bottom (P0 visual issue); security-training with_skill cover has '内训版 07 · INTERNAL' stamp rendering as text-on-text overlap
